page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 7.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory allocation of 3.5 GB per core. This instance does not include integrated GPU hardware or local storage, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants. With a memory-to-core ratio of 3.5 GB per vCPU, the hardware profile is optimized for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes this a cost-efficient option for entry-level compute tasks where external block storage is utilized.

Given its 2 vCPUs and 7.0 GB of RAM, the c4d-standard-2 is suited for lightweight compute workloads, microservices, small-scale web serving, and development environments. It is also appropriate for distributed application nodes that require dedicated CPU resources but have modest memory requirements.

It is suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, development environments, and small-scale application nodes.
